articles.title: Early reports of widespread damage as Cyclone Narelle smashes WA's Pilbara

children.content: Residents on WA's north-west coast are feeling the full brunt of severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it barrels across the region, tearing off roofs and flooding streets, with the system now downgraded to a category three.

image.altText: Trees blowing in wind, rain and fences down

articles.title: Track Cyclone Narelle's path and live wind speeds in WA

children.content: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and monitor live wind speeds as it crosses the WA coast.

image.altText: An illustration shows a cyclone icon above the Kimberley coast in the north of Western Australia

articles.title: Kakadu locals call for support following outstation floods

children.content: Kakadu residents whose homes have been submerged by roof-high floodwater said they feel like they've been forgotten, with evacuation centres and relief payments not made available to them.

image.altText: House in back left of image under water, halfway up its front porch, flood waters, small green trees in foreground.
Heavy rain and strongs winds lash homes in Exmouth.

children.content: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting the west Pilbara coast.

updates.title: Exmouth's evacuation centre roof partly blown off

items.title: The hidden uranium canisters that could end the Iran war

image.altText: A satellite image of various buildings in Iran.

children.content: A move from Israel or the United States to find and secure Iran's enriched uranium stockpile would be dangerous, but experts say it is one of the only ways to end the war and prevent it from returning.

items.title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to open Strait of Hormuz, takes swipe at Australia

children.content: US President Donald Trump singles out Australia while accusing a string of allies of failing to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, before extending Iran’s’ deadline to open the channel. Follow live.

image.altText: Strait of Hormuz pic

items.title: PM leaves door open to halving fuel excise temporarily

children.content: The prime minister leaves the door open to halving taxes on petrol and diesel, after the Coalition demands temporary relief from sky-high fuel prices.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese, wearing a suit and glasses, standing in front an Australian flag with his hand on his stomach.

items.title: Trump singles out Australia as he criticises allies on Iran war support

children.content: The US president singles out Australia as a country he was surprised was unwilling to help secure the Strait of Hormuz amid the war with Iran.

articles.title: Soaring diesel prices hit roads, housing and infrastructure builds

children.content: Industry groups have warned some infrastructure projects could stall without additional government support to cover fuel-related costs.

image.altText: Construction workers in high visibility clothing working on a construction site.

articles.title: Australian dependence on imported packaging exposed by Iran war

children.content: The war in Iran renews the government's interest in reducing Australia's dependence on imported packaging, particularly oil-reliant plastic.

image.altText: Bunches of apples and avocados wrapped in plastic and fruit netting in the supermarket

articles.title: Fuel not the only political problem posed by Middle East war

children.content: Fuel prices are the immediate issue for the Albanese government but it will also be under scrutiny for how it handles Australia's relationship with the United States and its volatile president.

image.altText: Chris Bowen in a suit, looking serious

items.title: Trump reveals Iran's 'great gift', plus what else you should know this morning

items.title: Is the end of the Iran war near?

children.content: Donald Trump says Iran wants a ceasefire deal. But Tehran says it's not even talking with the US. So what's going on and how likely is a ceasefire?

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

items.title: Inflation set to soar as fuel costs hit prices of everyday items from bread to ridesharing

children.content: Fuel surcharges are being applied across a range of industries, from the delivery of bricks to building sites to Australia's biggest rideshare companies, meaning consumer prices are rising.

image.altText: A man in a grocery store smiles at the camera.

items.title: ASX drops following sea of red on Wall St

children.content: The ASX falls at the open, following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Smashed businesses, the stink of dried mud: Life after Katherine floods

children.content: As it recovers from its worst flooding in decades, those living in the NT town of Katherine are trying to come to terms with the ongoing threat on their doorstep.

items.title: 'I've got a family to support': Sandilands's battle to stay on air in court

items.title: Nazeem Hussain developed his 'gut-punch' comedy over a lifetime of racism

children.content: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

items.title: Big Red Bash cancelled amid site change and rising fuel prices

children.content: This festival usually sees people go all out, spending thousands of dollars and travelling thousands of kilometres, but a potential change in location and fuel concerns have deterred ticket holders.

items.title: Craig's experience of abuse is not uncommon — but it is seldom talked about

children.content: Craig Hughes-Cashmore spent more than 20 years living with persistent suicidal thoughts, an experience that many share but rarely feel able to discuss.

children.content: Max Verstappen refuses to speak to media until Guardian journalist Giles Richards leaves because of a question he asked last year.

image.altText: Max Verstappen talks to a reporter.

items.title: 'Reliable evidence': Exercise halves early death risk of middle-aged women

children.content: Australian researchers found that middle-aged women who exercised regularly halved their risk of premature death. But most women aren't meeting physical activity guidelines.

image.altText: Woman jogging while listening music at beach against sky

items.title: Why millions are boycotting AI as debate rages over its ethical use

children.content: AI companies are stuck in a "perfect storm" of trying to control the use and output of their platforms while maintaining competitive profits, as millions of people boycott the technology, experts say.

image.altText: Male protesters walking in a group while holding 'Stop the AI Race' placards with red stop signs over 'AI'.

items.title: Where is Gus? Six months on, more questions than answers remain
